sections.paragraph: adj. dim, becoming dark, spoken of the eye, see the root, [which perhaps will give very little aid]: [“dark, dark-flashing, spoken of the eye”], Gen 49:12, חַכְלִילִי עֵינַיִם מִיַּיִן “being dim (as to his) eyes through wine,” which in this passage is to be taken in a good sense, as indicating plenty in the land of the tribe of Judah. [“Dark eyes are here contrasted with white teeth. Aquila well, κατάκοροι, satiated with colour, dark; LXX. χαροποιοὶ, Peshito ܙܪܳܓܰܢ shining, flashing, a word applied only to the eyes.” Ges. add.]
